entries.summary: Form-driven primary action (dbxActionForm + Approve) plus a nested ng-container dbxAction with dbxActionValue for a void Deny / Cancel button.
entries.hint: dbxActionForm + Approve, plus a nested dbxAction context for Deny.
entries.info:
The outer [dbxAction] hosts the primary work. A child component carries the dbxActionForm directive so the form's value (and validity / modified state) drives the Approve button.

The secondary button ( Deny ) lives inside a nested <ng-container dbxAction> . Angular DI looks up the nearest ancestor dbxAction for each dbxActionButton , so the inner button picks up the inner context, not the outer. dbxActionValue with no input supplies the empty payload that lets the button fire a void handler immediately.

Each context has its own dbxActionSnackbarError , so Approve / Deny feedback is independent.
entries.snippet:
@let decision = lastDecisionSignal(); @if (decision) {
@if (decision.outcome === 'approved') { Approved with reason: {{ decision.value.reason }} } @else { Denied. }
}

entries.summary: Bot status page — success color-accented status card, mat-table send history with tonal dbx-chip pills, and a hover-free manual-actions list card with a dashed generate box.
entries.hint: success color-accented status card, a mat-table send history with tonal dbx-chip pills, and a hover-free manual-actions card with a dashed generate box.
entries.info:
Composes a "bot status" page (lorem ipsum copy) out of a <dbx-section> wrapping three <mat-card> s — a full-width status card, a "Send history" card, and a "Manual actions" card — entirely from dbx-web utilities, with no component styles of its own.

The top status card carries both .dbx-card-color-accent and dbxColor="success" . The new .dbx-card-color-accent utility paints only the card's left edge from the bound color's --dbx-bg-color-current token, while the card body keeps its normal unpainted surface — the mat-card.dbx-color surface paint excludes this class through a zero-specificity :where(:not(...)) guard, so binding [dbxColor] here colors the edge without washing the whole card. The green status dot is the .dbx-dot utility (a currentColor circle) tinted with [dbxTextColor]="'success'" , and the warn "Pause Lorem Bot" button is a raised <dbx-button color="warn"> .

The "Send history" card uses a plain Angular Material <table mat-table> (the demo app's first MatTableModule use) styled purely with dbx typography utilities: uppercase, tracked, dim .dbx-text-label-medium header cells; a .dbx-text-title-small date over a dim .dbx-text-body-small time; a tonal <dbx-chip [small]> trigger pill (amber notice for manual sends, neutral grey for scheduled); a [dbxTextColor]="'primary'" remaining-count with a green [dbxTextColor]="'success'" delta (or a dim baseline hint); and a "Sent" <dbx-chip color="success"> that projects a leading .dbx-dot .

The "Manual actions" card reuses the hover-free .dbx-list-two-line-item list pattern: rows stay non-clickable and only the trailing button is interactive (raised + primary for the emphasized "Refresh" action, stroked otherwise). Because a dashed .dbx-content-border "Generate lorem content" box flows below the list, the list wrapper host carries dbx-list-auto-height so the .dbx-list sizes to its rows instead of filling the stretched card with its default height: 100% . Layout is responsive via Angular Flex Layout's CSS-Grid directives — gdColumns="5fr 4fr" with gdColumns.lt-lg="1fr" stacks the history and actions cards as the viewport narrows — and every button/link carries a ClickableAnchor whose onClick opens a MatSnackBar .

entries.summary: Dashboard overview page — dbx-section header with action buttons, dark inverse hero card with dbx-color-border stat pits, an accent-edged hover-free list card, and a responsive auto-fit row of stat cards.
entries.hint: dbx-section header with actions, dark inverse hero card with dbx-color-border stat pits, accent list card, and a responsive stat-card row.
entries.info:
Composes a dashboard "Overview" page out of a <dbx-section> whose [sectionHeader] slot projects the page-level action buttons to the right of the title, a dark hero <mat-card> , a "Worth knowing" <mat-card> with a hover-free list, and a responsive row of outlined stat cards.

The hero card is painted by the app-registered demo-inverse color template — [dbxColor]="{ template: 'demo-inverse' }" resolves to --mat-sys-inverse-surface / --mat-sys-inverse-on-surface via the DbxColorService , so the card flips correctly between light and dark themes without hard-coded colors. Its three stat boxes are <dbx-content-pit> blocks composed with the .dbx-color-border utility, which mixes a subtle 1px border from the inherited --dbx-color-current contrast color (override --dbx-color-border-tone / --dbx-color-border-width to tune it). Pits inside a [dbxColor] -painted surface automatically swap their default --mat-sys-surface-container background for a low wash of the same contrast variable (tune with --dbx-content-pit-color-tone ), secondary text uses the .dbx-color-text-dim utility, and the stat numbers use [dbxTextColor] configs ( --mat-sys-inverse-primary for the filled count) that stay legible on the inverse surface. The component declares no styles of its own — the card content fills the stretched card height via mat-card-content.dbx-flex-column + .dbx-flex-fill .

The "Worth knowing" card reuses the .dbx-list-two-line-item anchor-button list pattern: the list wrapper host applies dbx-list-no-hover-effects so rows render with no background or hover state, mapValuesToItemValues strips each row's anchor so the row itself stays non-clickable, and only the optional trailing <dbx-anchor> -wrapped stroked button is interactive. The card's blue left edge is the .dbx-card-accent utility (override --dbx-card-accent-color / --dbx-card-accent-border-width ).

Layout is responsive via Angular Flex Layout's CSS-Grid directives: gdColumns="2fr 1fr" with gdColumns.lt-md="1fr" stacks the hero and list cards below tablet width, and the bottom row's gdColumns="repeat(auto-fit, minmax(220px, 1fr))" reflows the four stat cards 4 → 2 → 1 as the viewport narrows. Every button and link carries a ClickableAnchor whose onClick opens a MatSnackBar so each action surface is demonstrably wired.

entries.summary: Single-select row of centered `.dbx-card-select` choice cards laid out with `dbxFlexGroup` + equal-width `[dbxFlexSize]` columns (equal-size as the screen shrinks, stacking below the `small` breakpoint). The selected `<mat-card appearance="outlined">` gets a tonal `[dbxColor]` wash, a matching themed border, and a round `<dbx-icon-tile [round]>` checkmark badge in the top-right; unselected cards stay neutral. Large glyphs are plain `<mat-icon>` that inherit the card's flipped text color.
entries.hint: Single-select row of equal-size centered choice cards (dbxFlexGroup) — the selected card gets a tonal [dbxColor] wash, a matching themed border, and a round checkmark badge.
entries.info:
A "selection" / "choice" card lets the user pick exactly one option from a small set, with the chosen card emphasised. Each option is a stock <mat-card appearance="outlined"> carrying the .dbx-card-select utility, which centers the card body in a column (a large <mat-icon> , a title, and a subtitle) and exposes a top-right slot for a checkmark badge via a .dbx-card-select-check child.

Selection is a single-select signal . The selected card receives [dbxColor] + [dbxColorTone]="16" , so the existing mat-card.dbx-color / .dbx-color-tonal rules paint the tonal background wash and flip the card text to the vibrant theme color — which the centered <mat-icon> (rendered in currentColor ) and the title inherit for free. The .dbx-card-selected modifier repaints the outlined-card border to that same --dbx-bg-color-current value, so the border matches the wash without any hard-coded colour.

The round checkmark is a <dbx-icon-tile [round]> carrying its own [dbxColor] (no tone), so it renders as a solid vibrant circle with a contrast-colour check. The unselected card omits [dbxColor] entirely, so it keeps the neutral outline + on-surface text. Each choice carries its own semantic color (a positive 'primary' , a destructive 'warn' , and a 'notice' review), so the emphasis colour follows the meaning of the option.

The options sit in a dbxFlexGroup row, each an equal-width [dbxFlexSize]="2" column, so the cards stay the same size and shrink in unison as the viewport narrows — and .dbx-card-select fills its column height ( height: 100% ) so they stay equal-height even if one subtitle wraps. Below the small breakpoint, [breakToColumn] stacks them into a single column.
entries.snippet:
Feedback Required
@for (choice of choices; track choice.key) {
@if (isSelected(choice.key)) { } {{ choice.icon }}
{{ choice.title }}
{{ choice.subtitle }}
}

entries.summary: Top-of-page hero card on an inverse surface — eyebrow, headline, progress bar, secondary CTA, and trailing percent.
entries.hint: Top-of-page hero on an inverse surface with eyebrow, headline, progress, action, and trailing percent.
entries.info:
A "hero" card is a high-emphasis module placed at the top of a page that summarises the user's most important task. This example uses Angular Material's <mat-card appearance="filled"> and only re-points the M3 --mat-card-filled-container-color token to --mat-sys-inverse-surface (with text painted from --mat-sys-inverse-on-surface ) so the card flips correctly between light/dark themes without any hard-coded colours.

Layout uses standard dbx utility classes — .dbx-p4 for the card body inset, .dbx-mb3 / .dbx-pb1 / .dbx-mb1 for vertical rhythm — instead of bespoke margin / padding values. Typography composes the M3 type-role utilities .dbx-text-label-medium (eyebrow), .dbx-text-title-large (headline), and .dbx-text-headline-large (trailing percent) with .dbx-uppercase / .dbx-tracked-wide for the eyebrow's overline treatment, so no inline font: declarations are needed. Inline emphasis uses semantic <strong> rather than a bespoke weight class.
entries.snippet:
YOUR ONBOARDING PROGRESS

{{ remaining }} requirements to go

{{ completed }} of {{ total }} requirements completed

{{ progressPercent }}%

entries.summary: Horizontally scrolling `.dbx-card-horizontal` row of fixed-width, equal-height outlined mat-cards — full-bleed `[dbxColor]` status header bars, an absolutely positioned `.dbx-card-horizontal-tab` "TODAY" chip (with scroll-clip-safe headroom), `.dbx-card-horizontal-detail` icon rows, `.dbx-card-horizontal-mini` recommendation rows, and bottom-anchored full-width footer CTAs — demonstrated as a week view of job day cards.
entries.hint: Fixed-width, equal-height outlined mat-cards in a .dbx-card-horizontal row that scrolls on the x-axis, with full-bleed [dbxColor] status headers and bottom-anchored footer CTAs.
entries.info:
A .dbx-card-horizontal wrapper lays out <mat-card appearance="outlined" class="dbx-card-horizontal-item"> cards as a fixed-width ( --dbx-card-horizontal-item-width , 300px default), equal-height row that scrolls horizontally instead of wrapping. Each card's CTA lives in <mat-card-footer> , which the item utility anchors to the card bottom with margin-top: auto — so the buttons stay on one line even though the cards carry different amounts of content.

The status bar is a plain <div class="dbx-card-horizontal-header"> placed before <mat-card-content> : mat-card children stack in a padding-less flex column, so the bar spans the card edge-to-edge with no negative margins, and its top corners inherit the card radius. It is painted entirely by [dbxColor] — solid 'primary' for ASSIGNED (the check icon follows currentColor ), tonal 'notice' + [dbxColorTone] for OPEN, whose .dbx-card-horizontal-header-dot also rides currentColor .

The "TODAY" tab is a <dbx-chip [tone]="100" [small]="true"> with the .dbx-card-horizontal-tab utility, absolutely positioned to poke above the card by --dbx-card-horizontal-tab-overlap . Because the row's overflow-x: auto scroll-clips anything outside its padding box, the row reserves that same overlap as top padding — one token drives both, so the tab can never be clipped. The today card also toggles .dbx-card-horizontal-item-highlight , repainting the outlined-card border via --mat-outlined-card-outline-color (the same pattern as .dbx-card-select ).

Body content is composed from existing utilities: .dbx-card-horizontal-detail icon rows, .dbx-card-horizontal-divider hairlines, .dbx-card-horizontal-mini recommendation rows, eyebrow labels from .dbx-text-label-small .dbx-uppercase .dbx-tracked-wide , themed text from [dbxTextColor] , and a <dbx-icon-tile [round]> + <dbx-chip> empty state.

entries.summary: Two-line list whose trailing button wraps a per-item ClickableAnchor via dbx-anchor.
entries.hint: Two-line row whose trailing button wraps a per-item ClickableAnchor via dbx-anchor.
entries.info:
Each item carries its own anchor: ClickableAnchor field. The item template wraps the trailing <button mat-flat-button> in <dbx-anchor [anchor]="anchor"> so clicking the button fires whatever the data dictates — an onClick callback, a router ref , or an external url .

Because mapValuesToItemValues drops anchor off the row item, the row's hosting (mat-list-item) doesn't pick it up — only the trailing button is interactive. Use this when each item already knows where it routes and you want the action visually scoped to a button.

For the alternative — a whole-row click target supplied by the host rather than embedded in data — see list-anchor-row-modifier .

Last clicked: {{ clickedKey() ?? '(none)' }}

entries.summary: Two-line list where dbxListItemAnchorModifier makes the entire row a click target — data has no anchor field.
entries.hint: Whole-row click target wired by dbxListItemAnchorModifier — the data has no anchor field of its own.
entries.info:
The list values don't carry an anchor field. Instead, the host attaches dbxListItemModifier [dbxListItemAnchorModifier]="makeClickAnchor" on the wrapper. The modifier runs the host's AnchorForValueFunction for each item, sets the resulting ClickableAnchor on the DbxValueListItem , and the list's row hosting wires it as a row click — the entire row becomes the click target without the data needing to know anything about routing.

Pick this pattern when the routing target is computed from the value's identity (e.g. a router ref built from itemValue.key ) and you want the row itself to be the action surface — no trailing button needed; a chevron is plenty of affordance.

Last clicked: {{ clickedKey() ?? '(none)' }}

entries.summary: Two-line list with template-painted icon tile, progress bar, and trailing button.
entries.hint: Two-line row with a template-painted icon tile, full-width progress bar, and trailing action button.
entries.info:
This variant demonstrates a heavier, custom item where the template paints the leading icon itself instead of relying on the parent list view. Add the .dbx-list-two-line-item-with-icon modifier so the parent skips its own icon, then drop the mapped item's icon field to suppress the built-in slot.

The leading element uses the reusable <dbx-icon-tile> component — a rounded, padded container that takes an [icon] input and pairs with [dbxColor] + [dbxColorTone] for a tonal background. Inside .item-left the template stacks a title, an X of Y complete details line, and a <mat-progress-bar> that spans the full width up to the trailing column; .item-right holds a flat action button.

Supporting utilities used here: .dbx-list-item-padded-thick for a roomier row, .dbx-list-card-items-list on the wrapper for the tinted/rounded card surface — applied to the actual .mat-mdc-list-item so hover/focus state-layers stay within the visible card boundary (defaults flow through --mat-sys-surface-container + --mat-sys-corner-large ; overridable via --dbx-list-item-card-background / --dbx-list-item-card-border-radius , with inter-card spacing exposed as --dbx-list-card-items-list-gap ), .dbx-list-item-p0 on the view item host to drop Material's default list-item padding (so the icon tile can own its leading inset), and .dbx-list-no-hover-effects on the wrapper to disable the hover state-layer and pointer cursor for non-clickable rows.

entries.summary: Two-line list with parent-rendered leading icon and trailing status chip.
entries.hint: Two-line row with parent-rendered leading icon, title/details/footnote, and a trailing status chip.
entries.info:
The .dbx-list-two-line-item wrapper organizes a row into .item-left (column with .item-title , .item-details , and .item-details-footnote ) and .item-right (trailing content). Pair with .dbx-list-item-padded for the standard list-row padding and min-height.

This example uses the default selection-list rendering: the parent list view paints the leading icon (it reads icon off each mapped item), so the item template only fills .item-left with the title/details/footnote and .item-right with a <dbx-chip> for the status badge.

entries.summary: Outlined mat-card status banner with `[dbxColor]` + `[dbxColorTone]` painting the tonal background. The `.dbx-card-banner` utility lays out a `.dbx-card-banner-header` row inside `<mat-card-content>` (skipping mat-card-header) pairing a vibrant-colored `<dbx-icon-tile [round]>` avatar with a `<mat-card-title>` plus a `<mat-card-footer>` wrap-row of action buttons — toggles between an active-chat (notice) and ending-chat (warn) variant.
entries.hint: Outlined mat-card with [dbxColor] tonal background, mat-card-header icon + title, content body, and a footer action row.
entries.info:
Reimplements the "you're already in a chat" / "your chat is wrapping up" status banner from a downstream support page using stock Material card slots plus dbx-web color theming. The host is <mat-card class="dbx-card-banner" appearance="outlined" [dbxColor]="view.color" [dbxColorTone]="18"> . The dbx-web card stylesheet contributes two pieces here: a generic mat-card.dbx-color rule that re-emits the dbx-color background paint at higher specificity than MDC's .mat-mdc-card background-color (so the tonal wash actually shows through every appearance variant), and the .dbx-card-banner utility that centers an <mat-icon mat-card-avatar> and turns <mat-card-footer> into a wrapping action row.

<mat-card-header> is dropped on purpose — its default padding made the banner feel tall. Instead a <dbx-icon-tile> + <mat-card-title> row lives inside <mat-card-content> via a .dbx-card-banner-header child (centered flex row, gap from --dbx-padding-3 ), the body paragraph follows directly underneath, and the action buttons live in <mat-card-footer> . The icon tile passes [dbxColor]="view.color" with [round]="true" so it renders as a solid circular avatar in the vibrant theme color, popping out of the tonal banner without any per-component CSS. The .dbx-card-banner utility supplies the header-row flex and the footer wrap-row spacing + padding. The toggle controls at the top reuse the existing .dbx-button-wrap-group utility.

The "Open chat" action passes [color]="view.color" so its label tracks the banner's vibrant color while keeping a transparent background that lets the tonal wash show through; "Dismiss" stays as a basic <dbx-button> . Action clicks pop a MatSnackBar so the demo proves the wiring without depending on routing or chat infrastructure.

The kindSignal swaps the entire view between the two presets without re-templating, modelling the real-world pattern where the banner is driven by a derived store selector.
entries.snippet:
@if (viewSignal(); as view) {
{{ view.title }}

{{ view.text }}

entries.summary: Static support landing page — outlined hero card with a dbx-button CTA and search input plus a responsive 3-column grid of topic mat-cards whose rows are dbx-anchor onClick handlers that pop a snackbar.
entries.hint: Page header + hero CTA/search card + responsive 3-column grid of topic cards.
entries.info:
Composes a marketing-style support page out of Angular Material cards plus dbx-web's <dbx-button> , <dbx-anchor> , and <dbx-icon-tile> : an outlined hero <mat-card> for the search/CTA row and a responsive grid of outlined topic <mat-card> boxes whose rows are individual <dbx-anchor [anchor]> click targets.

The topic grid uses Angular Flex Layout's CSS-Grid directives — gdColumns="repeat(auto-fit, minmax(320px, 1fr))" + gdGap — so the cards auto-collapse 3 → 2 → 1 columns as the viewport narrows, with no breakpoint media queries or wrapping fxFlex shells needed. The hero CTA + search row uses fxLayout="row" with fxLayout.lt-md="column" so it stacks below the tablet breakpoint. The CTA itself composes the .dbx-grow-button + .dbx-button-big utilities (full-width, taller mat-button) instead of hand-rolled ::ng-deep width / height overrides.

Topic-card avatars are <dbx-icon-tile [round]> with dbxColor="primary" + [dbxColorTone]="18" for the tonal swatch. Each card body is a plain <ul> / <li> so the browser's default disc marker supplies the bullet, and topic links use [dbxTextColor]="'primary'" for the link color plus a <span class="dbx-link-hover"> wrapper around the projected label for the hover-only-underline affordance. The .dbx-link-hover / .dbx-link utilities pair with .dbx-u — .dbx-link is always underlined, .dbx-link-hover underlines on hover, both keep a pointer cursor. The class lives on the projected <span> rather than the <dbx-anchor> host because dbx-anchor 's internal <a class="dbx-anchor-a"> bakes in text-decoration: none and would strip a host-level .dbx-link .

Each topic row supplies a ClickableAnchor with an onClick callback to dbx-anchor ; the callback opens a MatSnackBar with a "Dismiss" action so every link demonstrably triggers a button-bearing popup. Swap the onClick for a ref / url to route or open externally instead.
